yaml.title: References
yaml.subtitle: The reading list behind the methods on this site

markdown.markdown:
The references below are the canonical methods papers for APIM
work, plus the two applied works whose data structures the
simulated datasets emulate. Each entry has a one-line note on
*when* to read it.

::: {.callout-tip}
The first 8 entries are *methods* references on APIM and the R
packages used throughout this site. The last 2 are the *primary
textbook* and the *applied example* whose data structure our
simulated data emulate. The remaining entries are auxiliary
references for specific extensions.
:::

## Foundational APIM papers

::: {.ref-item}
**Kenny, D. A., Kashy, D. A., & Cook, W. L. (2006).** *Dyadic data
analysis.* Guilford Press.

The primary APIM textbook — the conceptual and analytic basis for
the site. Read chapters 1–4 for the foundations, then dip into the
chapters on specific analytic approaches as you need them.
:::

::: {.ref-item}
**Cook, W. L., & Kenny, D. A. (2005).** The Actor–Partner
Interdependence Model: A model of bidirectional effects in
developmental studies. *International Journal of Behavioral
Development, 29*(2), 101–108.

The foundational paper that articulated APIM in psychology.
:::

::: {.ref-item}
**Kenny, D. A., & Ledermann, T. (2010).** Detecting, measuring, and
testing dyadic patterns in the Actor–Partner Interdependence Model.
*Journal of Family Psychology, 24*(3), 359–366.

Introduces the [k-pattern tests](../foundations/kpatterns.html)
(actor-only, couple, contrast) used to interpret APIM effects.
:::

## Handbook chapters and practical introductions

::: {.ref-item}
**Kashy, D. A., Donnellan, M. B., & Ackerman, R. A. (2017).** Dyadic
analysis. In A. L. Vangelisti & D. Perlman (Eds.), *The Cambridge
handbook of personal relationships* (2nd ed., pp. 101–115). Cambridge
University Press.

Modern, accessible handbook chapter covering dyadic data analysis
end-to-end.
:::

::: {.ref-item}
**Ackerman, R. A., Donnellan, M. B., & Kashy, D. A. (2011).** Working
with dyadic data: An introduction. In L. M. Horowitz & S. N. Strack
(Eds.), *Handbook of interpersonal psychology: Theory, research,
assessment, and therapeutic interventions* (pp. 547–558). Wiley.

Concise, practical introduction with worked examples — a good
first methods read.
:::

## SEM specification for indistinguishable dyads

::: {.ref-item}
**Olsen, J. A., & Kenny, D. A. (2006).** Structural equation modeling
with interchangeable dyads. *Psychological Methods, 11*(2), 127–141.

SEM specification for *indistinguishable* dyads — underpins the
wide-format `lavaan` syntax in the [SEM wide
tutorial](../tutorials/indistinguishable/sem.html).
:::

## Worked SEM tutorial (k-pattern tests)

::: {.ref-item}
**Fitzpatrick, J., Gareau, A., Lafontaine, M.-F., & Gaudreau, P.
(2016).** How to use the Actor–Partner Interdependence Model to
estimate different dyadic patterns in Mplus: A step-by-step tutorial.
*Tutorials in Quantitative Methods for Psychology, 12*(1), 74–86.

Worked SEM tutorial on the k-pattern tests; closely parallels the
[`lavaan` scripts in the SEM wide
tutorial](../tutorials/distinguishable/sem-wide.html) (despite the
Mplus label, the model specification is identical).
:::

## R implementation

::: {.ref-item}
**Rosseel, Y. (2012).** lavaan: An R package for structural equation
modeling. *Journal of Statistical Software, 48*(2), 1–36.

The R-side SEM engine used throughout this site.
:::

::: {.ref-item}
**Bates, D., Mächler, M., Bolker, B., & Walker, S. (2015).** Fitting
linear mixed-effects models using lme4. *Journal of Statistical
Software, 67*(1), 1–48.

The R-side multilevel modelling engine used throughout this site.
:::

::: {.ref-item}
**Kuznetsova, A., Brockhoff, P. B., & Christensen, R. H. B. (2017).**
lmerTest package: Tests in linear mixed effects models. *Journal of
Statistical Software, 82*(13), 1–26.

`lmerTest` provides p-values and profile-likelihood confidence
intervals for `lme4` models.
:::

## Extension: intensive longitudinal / diary designs

::: {.ref-item}
**Bolger, N., & Laurenceau, J.-P. (2013).** *Intensive longitudinal
methods: An introduction to diary and experience sampling research.*
Guilford Press.

Methods reference for the diary / ESM extension of APIM (days
within persons within dyads).
:::

## Applied example (the data structure our scripts emulate)

::: {.ref-item}
**Hahn, V. C., Binnewies, C., & Dormann, C. (2014).** The role of
partners and children for employees' daily recovery. *Journal of
Vocational Behavior, 85*(1), 39–48.

The applied example our scripts emulate — reproduced in the
[Hahn et al. (2014) replication
tutorial](../tutorials/moderated/hahn2014.html).
:::

## Local materials

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| [`scripts/01_simulate_data.R`](https://github.com/michaelides/apim/blob/master/scripts/01_simulate_data.R) | Data generation for the tutorial dataset |
| [`scripts/02_indistinguishable_mlm.R`](https://github.com/michaelides/apim/blob/master/scripts/02_indistinguishable_mlm.R) | Indistinguishable dyads, MLM |
| [`scripts/03_indistinguishable_sem.R`](https://github.com/michaelides/apim/blob/master/scripts/03_indistinguishable_sem.R) | Indistinguishable dyads, SEM (wide format, `lavaan`) |
| [`scripts/04_distinguishable_mlm.R`](https://github.com/michaelides/apim/blob/master/scripts/04_distinguishable_mlm.R) | Distinguishable dyads, MLM with gender moderation |
| [`scripts/05_distinguishable_sem_moderation.R`](https://github.com/michaelides/apim/blob/master/scripts/05_distinguishable_sem_moderation.R) | Distinguishable dyads, SEM with gender moderation |
| [`scripts/06_distinguishable_sem_wide.R`](https://github.com/michaelides/apim/blob/master/scripts/06_distinguishable_sem_wide.R) | Distinguishable dyads, SEM wide format |
| [`scripts/07_two_intercept_models.R`](https://github.com/michaelides/apim/blob/master/scripts/07_two_intercept_models.R) | Two-intercept (actor–partner) models |
| [`scripts/08_moderated_apim.R`](https://github.com/michaelides/apim/blob/master/scripts/08_moderated_apim.R) | Moderated APIM, replicating Hahn, Binnewies, & Dormann (2014) |
| [`exercises/`](https://github.com/michaelides/apim/blob/master/exercises) | Student exercises |
